# Hooks

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

`hooks/` contains runtime hook scripts and in-process helper modules for startup context, prompt-time skill advice, compaction support, memory surfacing, mutation feedback, and response hints.

Current state:

- Runtime startup hooks transport compact startup context for Claude and Codex.
- Prompt-time hooks call the native advisor and inject a brief through each runtime surface.
- `index.ts` exports helper modules used inside MCP responses and mutation flows.
- OpenCode prompt-time advice is delivered by the OpenCode plugin and bridge, not by a subfolder in this directory.

---

## 2. ARCHITECTURE

```text
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                              HOOKS                               │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

┌────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐
│ Claude         │ ───▶ │ runtime hook files │ ───▶ │ startup or prompt   │
│ Codex          │      │ per runtime        │      │ context transport  │
└────────────────┘      └─────────┬──────────┘      └─────────┬──────────┘
                                  │                           │
                                  ▼                           ▼
                         ┌────────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐
                         │ native advisor     │      │ managed context    │
                         │ and startup brief  │      │ stdout, file, hook  │
                         └─────────┬──────────┘      └─────────┬──────────┘
                                   │                           │
                                   ▼                           ▼
                         ┌────────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐
                         │ index.ts exports   │ ───▶ │ MCP envelope hints │
                         │ helper modules     │      │ and cache feedback │
                         └────────────────────┘      └────────────────────┘

Dependency direction: runtime folders ───▶ shared helpers ───▶ MCP response metadata.
```

---

## 3. DIRECTORY TREE

```text
mcp_server/hooks/
├── claude/                        # Claude session, prompt, compaction, and transcript hooks
├── codex/                         # Codex session, prompt, pre-tool, and wrapper hooks
├── code-index-cli-fallback.ts      # Warm-only code-index CLI fallback helper
├── index.ts                       # Public helper exports
├── memory-surface.ts              # Context extraction and constitutional cache helpers
├── mutation-feedback.ts           # Post-mutation feedback payloads
├── response-hints.ts              # Auto-surface hints and token count sync
├── shared-provenance.ts           # Provenance-wrapped transport helpers
├── spec-memory-cli-fallback.ts     # Warm-only spec-memory CLI fallback helper
├── warm-cli-fallback-envelope.ts   # Bounded warm CLI fallback envelope helpers
└── README.md
```

---

## 4. KEY FILES

| File or directory | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `claude/` | Claude runtime hook scripts and README. |
| `codex/` | Codex native hook scripts plus prompt-wrapper fallback. |
| `code-index-cli-fallback.ts` | Bounded warm-only CLI recovery for code-index hook contexts. |
| `index.ts` | Public export barrel for in-process helper modules. |
| `memory-surface.ts` | Extracts context hints and surfaces constitutional or triggered memory. |
| `mutation-feedback.ts` | Maps `MutationHookResult` values into public `postMutationHooks` response payloads. |
| `response-hints.ts` | Adds auto-surface hints and token counts to MCP JSON envelopes. |
| `shared-provenance.ts` | Wraps hook transport with provenance metadata. |
| `spec-memory-cli-fallback.ts` | Bounded warm-only CLI recovery for spec-memory hook contexts. |
| `warm-cli-fallback-envelope.ts` | Shared response envelope for warm CLI fallback attempts. |

---

## 5. BOUNDARIES AND FLOW

| Boundary | Rule |
|---|---|
| Runtime scripts | Keep runtime registration details in each runtime subfolder and README. |
| In-process helpers | Export shared helper modules through `index.ts`. |
| Startup transport | Use startup hooks for compact context priming, not prompt-time advisor delivery. |
| Prompt advice | Use prompt-time hooks or the OpenCode plugin bridge for advisor briefs. |
| Mutation feedback | Read mutation results from `../handlers/mutation-hooks.ts` before building public payloads. |
| Response contract | Expose post-mutation UX state through the `postMutationHooks` field, preserving the `MutationHookResult` cache-clearing and error fields. |

Main flow:

```text
╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Runtime event starts or prompt submits   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
                  │
                  ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Runtime-specific hook script runs         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
                  ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Startup brief or advisor brief is built  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
                  ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Runtime transport injects compact output │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
                  ▼
╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Agent receives context or advisor brief  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

---

## 6. ENTRYPOINTS

| Entrypoint | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `claude/session-prime.ts` | Hook script | Claude startup context injection. |
| `codex/session-start.ts` | Hook script | Codex native session-start injection. |
| `*/user-prompt-submit.ts` | Hook script | Prompt-time skill advisor delivery for supported runtimes. |
| `index.ts` | Module | Public exports for in-process helper functions. |

Main helper exports include `extractContextHint`, `getConstitutionalMemories`, `clearConstitutionalCache`, `autoSurfaceMemories`, `autoSurfaceAtToolDispatch`, `autoSurfaceAtCompaction`, `MEMORY_AWARE_TOOLS`, `buildMutationHookFeedback`, `appendAutoSurfaceHints`, `syncEnvelopeTokenCount`, and `serializeEnvelopeWithTokenCount`.

---

## 7. VALIDATION

Run from `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server` unless noted.

```bash
npx vitest run hooks
```

Expected result: hook helper and runtime hook tests exit with Vitest success.

---

## 8. RELATED

- [`../handlers/README.md`](../handlers/README.md)
- [`../core/README.md`](../core/README.md)
- [`../../references/hooks/skill_advisor_hook.md`](../../references/hooks/skill_advisor_hook.md)
- [`../../references/config/hook_system.md`](../../references/config/hook_system.md)

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
3: // MODULE: Spec Memory CLI
4: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
5: // Daemon-backed command line client for the mk-spec-memory tool
6: // surface. The CLI is intentionally IPC-only: all persistence remains
7: // owned by the already-running context server daemon.
8:

12: import { createRequire } from 'node:module';

36: type OutputFormat = 'json' | 'text' | 'jsonl';

102:   }

110: }

113:   constructor(message: string) {
114:     super(message);
115:     this.name = 'CliProtocolError';
116:   }
117: }
118: 
119: class JsonRpcError extends Error {
120:   public readonly code?: number;
121:   public readonly data?: unknown;
122: 
123:   constructor(error: NonNullable<JsonRpcResponse['error']>) {
124:     super(error.message ?? 'JSON-RPC request failed');
125:     this.name = 'JsonRpcError';
126:     this.code = error.code;
127:     this.data = error.data;
128:   }
129: }
130: 
131: // Awaits drain when the kernel pipe buffer is full: a fire-and-forget
132: // write followed by process.exit() truncates large payloads at the pipe
133: // buffer boundary (observed as exactly 64KB on darwin). Streams without
134: // emitter methods (test fakes) resolve immediately.
135: async function writeLine(stream: Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'>, value: string): Promise<void> {
136:   const flushed = stream.write(`${value}\n`);
137:   if (flushed) return;
138:   const drainable = stream as Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'> & Partial<Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'once' | 'off'>>;
139:   if (typeof drainable.once !== 'function') return;
140:   await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
141:     const settle = (): void => {
142:       drainable.off?.('drain', settle);
143:       drainable.off?.('error', settle);
144:       drainable.off?.('close', settle);
145:       resolve();
146:     };
147:     drainable.once?.('drain', settle);
148:     drainable.once?.('error', settle);
149:     drainable.once?.('close', settle);
150:   });
151: }
152: 
153: function currentModulePath(): string {
154:   return fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
155: }
156: 
157: function findRepoPaths(startFile = currentModulePath()): RepoPaths {
158:   let current = path.dirname(startFile);
159: 
160:   while (true) {
161:     const directOpencodeDir = path.basename(current) === '.opencode' ? current : path.join(current, '.opencode');
162:     const launcherPath = path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'bin', 'mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs');
163:     const bridgePath = path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'bin', 'lib', 'launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs');
164:     if (existsSync(launcherPath) && existsSync(bridgePath)) {
165:       const repoRoot = path.dirname(directOpencodeDir);
166:       return {
167:         opencodeDir: directOpencodeDir,
168:         repoRoot,
169:         launcherPath,
170:         bridgePath,
171:         dbDir: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database'),
172:         packageJsonPath: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'package.json'),
173:       };
174:     }
175: 
176:     const parent = path.dirname(current);
177:     if (parent === current) {
178:       throw new CliProtocolError('Unable to locate .opencode/bin launcher assets from CLI path');
179:     }
180:     current = parent;
181:   }
182: }
183: 
184: function loadBridge(paths: RepoPaths): BridgeModule {
185:   const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
186:   return require(paths.bridgePath) as BridgeModule;
187: }
188: 
189: function readCliVersion(paths: RepoPaths): string {
190:   const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
191:   const pkg = require(paths.packageJsonPath) as { version?: unknown };
192:   return typeof pkg.version === 'string' ? pkg.version : '0.0.0';
193: }
194: 
195: function toKebabCommand(name: string): string {
196:   return name.replace(/_/g, '-');
197: }
198: 
199: function toCamelCommand(name: string): string {
200:   return name.replace(/_([a-z0-9])/g, (_match, char: string) => char.toUpperCase());
201: }
202: 
203: function commandAliases(name: string): string[] {
204:   return Array.from(new Set([name, toKebabCommand(name), toCamelCommand(name)]));
205: }
206: 
207: function usageText(command?: string): string {
208:   const tool = command ? getToolDefinition(command) : null;
209:   if (tool) {
210:     return `spec-memory ${tool.name}
211: 
212: Description:
213:   ${tool.description}
214: 
215: Aliases:
216:   ${commandAliases(tool.name).join(', ')}
217: 
218: Input schema:
219: ${JSON.stringify(tool.inputSchema, null, 2)}`;
220:   }
221: 
222:   return `spec-memory - daemon-backed CLI for mk-spec-memory
223: 
224: Usage:
225:   spec-memory list-tools [--format json|text|jsonl] [--compact|--names-only]
226:   spec-memory completion bash|zsh
227:   spec-memory <tool_name> [--json '{...}'] [--format json|text|jsonl] [--timeout-ms N] [--session-id ID] [--warm-only]
228:   spec-memory <tool_name> --param value [--another-param value]
229: 
230: Examples:

233:   spec-memory completion zsh

235:   spec-memory memory_search --query "gold query battery" --limit 3
236:   spec-memory memory_context --json '{"input":"resume", "mode":"resume"}'
237: 
238: Exit codes:

240: 
241: Exit 69 recovery:

243:     - Backend protocol version changed: rebuild the CLI and daemon from the same checkout, then update any pinned client.
244:     - Socket path or daemon config mismatch: check SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR, socket length, and daemon config before retrying.`;
245: }

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR ROUTER — CANONICAL ROUTE MANIFEST
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Source of truth for /doctor <target> dispatch.
# Read by: .opencode/commands/doctor/speckit.md (the router)
# Validated by: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/route-validate.sh
# Consumed by: Skill Advisor lexical lane (per-target trigger_phrases)
#
# Schema (per route):
#   target:           string — positional name the user types
#   yaml:             string — YAML asset filename in assets/
#   setup_vars:       list   — variables the router resolves and passes to YAML
#   allowed_flags:    list   — per-target flag schema (target-first parsing)
#   mutating:         enum   — read-only | add-only | mutates
#   gate3_location:   string — concrete mutation target (or "n/a" for read-only)
#   mcp_tools:        list   — subset of router's frontmatter allowed-tools union
#   trigger_phrases:  list   — advisor lexical-routing phrases for this target
#
# Code-graph implementation/docs are owned by .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/;
# stable code_graph/detect_changes MCP tools are registered through mk-code-index.
# Skill-advisor recommendation tools are registered through mk-skill-advisor.
# To add a new target: append a route here, then re-run route-validate.sh.
# To remove a target: delete the route AND its corresponding assets/doctor_<target>.yaml.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
schema_version: 1
routes:
  - target: memory
    yaml: doctor_memory.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode, intent, incremental, no_snapshot, dry_run]
    allowed_flags: ["--incremental=true|false", "--no-snapshot", "--dry-run"]
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (read-only diagnostic; reports drift and recommends action, no DB writes)"
    mcp_tools:
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_health
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_index_scan
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_drift_why
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_search
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_stats
    script_invocations:
      - 'Warm-only: probe the existing mk-spec-memory IPC socket first; only when it answers, run node .opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs memory_health --format json --timeout-ms 500 --warm-only (the flag guarantees no daemon start; exit 75 = backend unavailable, retryable)'
    trigger_phrases:
      - "memory continuity index drift"
      - "memory health rebuild"
      - "context-index doctor"
      - "voyage embedding rebuild"

  - target: embeddings
    yaml: doctor_embeddings.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode]
    allowed_flags: []
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (read-only provider and model-server status)"
    mcp_tools:
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__embedder_status
    trigger_phrases:
      - "embedding provider status"
      - "hf-local model-server health"
      - "embedder_status diagnostics"

  - target: causal-graph
    yaml: doctor_causal-graph.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode, intent, confidence_threshold, no_snapshot, dry_run]
    allowed_flags: ["--confidence-threshold=N", "--no-snapshot", "--dry-run"]
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (read-only diagnostic; samples causal_edges and recommends candidates, no writes)"
    mcp_tools:
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_causal_stats
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_drift_why
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_search
    trigger_phrases:
      - "causal edges drift"
      - "causal graph doctor"
      - "lineage coverage"
      - "memory_causal_stats"

  - target: code-graph
    yaml: doctor_code-graph.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode, scope]
    allowed_flags:
      - "--scope=stale|missed|bloat|all|excludes"
      - "--operation=rescan|prune-excludes|repair-nodes|recover-sqlite-corruption|rollback-bad-apply"
      - "--dry-run"
      - "--confirm"
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (Phase A diagnostic is read-only; emits an exclude/language report, never scans or writes the index)"
    mcp_tools:
      - mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_status
      - mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_query
      - mcp__mk_code_index__code_graph_context
      - mcp__mk_code_index__detect_changes
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_context
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_search
    script_invocations:
      - 'Warm-only: probe the existing mk-code-index IPC socket first; only when it answers, run node .opencode/bin/code-index.cjs code_graph_status --format json --timeout-ms 500 --warm-only (the flag guarantees no daemon start; exit 75 = backend unavailable, retryable)'
    trigger_phrases:
      - "code-graph index drift"
      - "code-graph rebuild"
      - "code-graph stale excludes"
      - "code-graph corruption recover"

  - target: deep-loop
    yaml: doctor_deep-loop.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode, intent, scope, no_snapshot, dry_run]
    allowed_flags: ["--scope=research|review|council|both|all", "--no-snapshot", "--dry-run"]
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (read-only diagnostic; reads coverage + council graphs and iteration folders + ai-council artifacts, never upserts)"
    mcp_tools: []
    script_invocations:
      - 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/status.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "{loop_type}" --session-id "{session_id}"'
      - 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/query.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "{loop_type}" --session-id "{session_id}" --query-type "{query_type}"'
      - 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "{loop_type}" --session-id "{session_id}"'
    trigger_phrases:
      - "deep-loop coverage"
      - "deep-loop graph rebuild"
      - "deep-research convergence drift"
      - "deep-review iteration graph"

  - target: skill-advisor
    yaml: doctor_skill-advisor.yaml
    setup_vars: [execution_mode, scope, skip_tests, dry_run]
    allowed_flags: ["--skip-tests", "--dry-run", "--scope=all|explicit|derived|lexical"]
    mutating: mutates
    gate3_location: "lib/scorer/lanes/*.ts + .opencode/skills/*/graph-metadata.json"
    mcp_tools:
      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__advisor_recommend
      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__advisor_status
      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__advisor_validate
      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__advisor_rebuild
      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__skill_graph_scan

      - mcp__mk_skill_advisor__skill_graph_status
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_context
      - mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_search
    script_invocations:
      - 'Warm-only: probe the existing mk-skill-advisor IPC socket first; only when it answers, run node .opencode/bin/skill-advisor.cjs advisor_status --workspace-root "$PWD" --format json --timeout-ms 500 --warm-only (the flag guarantees no daemon start; exit 75 = backend unavailable, retryable)'

      - "skill_graph scan"

    mutating: read-only

    trigger_phrases:

      - "description budget check"
      - "skill char count drift"
      - "frontmatter description trim"

  - target: parent-skill

    allowed_flags: ["--dir=<path>"]
    mutating: read-only
    gate3_location: "n/a (read-only structural audit; inspects a parent skill's graph-metadata/registry and reports, no writes)"
    mcp_tools: []   # script-only structural audit; no MCP calls

    trigger_phrases:

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPANION COMMAND ROUTES (/doctor:mcp sub-actions)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# /doctor:mcp dispatches via its own command (.opencode/commands/doctor/mcp.md).
# These rows are tracked here for advisor + CI visibility only — the mcp.md
# command parses its own sub-action argv and dispatches to the right YAML.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mcp_subroutes:
  - sub_action: install
    yaml: doctor_mcp_install.yaml
    allowed_flags: ["--server <name>", "--runtime <name>"]
    mutating: mutates
    gate3_location: "MCP server install dirs + runtime config (.opencode.json / .claude/settings.json / .codex/config.toml)"
    trigger_phrases:

      - "MCP server fresh install"
      - "reinstall MCP"

  - sub_action: debug
    yaml: doctor_mcp_debug.yaml
    allowed_flags: ["--fix", "--server <name>"]
    mutating: mutates
    gate3_location: "MCP server logs + runtime config repair"
    trigger_phrases:
      - "MCP server broken"
      - "MCP debug"
      - "MCP connection failure"
      - "mcp-doctor.sh"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ORCHESTRATOR (NOT routed through /doctor — kept here for reference)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# /doctor:update is a STANDALONE command at .opencode/commands/doctor/update.md.
# It does NOT route through /doctor. This block exists for advisor visibility.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
standalone:

    yaml: doctor_update.yaml
    mutating: mutates
    trigger_phrases:
      - "spec-kit version migration"
      - "doctor update orchestrator"
      - "rebuild all spec-kit databases"
      - "doctor full sync"

739: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
740: // 1. PROVIDER RESOLUTION
741: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
742: 
743: /**
744:  * Resolve provider based on env vars. Local-first precedence:
745:  * 1) explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER (the only way to auto-select cloud),
746:  * 2) persisted vec_metadata active Ollama pointer,
747:  * 3) hf-local local fallback.
748:  * Cloud (OpenAI/Voyage) is never auto-selected; it is reached only via an explicit
749:  * EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER, or as a last-resort cascade when hf-local creation fails
750:  * (getCascadeFallbackOrder). Mirrors the bootstrap auto-select cascade order
751:  * ollama -> hf-local -> openai -> voyage.
752:  */
753: export function resolveProvider(): ProviderResolution {
754:   const explicitProvider = getExplicitProviderOverride();
755:   if (explicitProvider && explicitProvider !== 'auto') {
756:     return {
757:       name: explicitProvider,
758:       reason: 'Explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER variable',
759:     };
760:   }
761: 
762:   const activeOllamaEmbedder = resolveActiveOllamaEmbedder();
763:   if (activeOllamaEmbedder) {
764:     return {
765:       name: 'ollama',
766:       reason: `vec_metadata active_embedder_name=${activeOllamaEmbedder.name} (${activeOllamaEmbedder.dim}-dim)`,

778:     name: 'hf-local',

786:  * post-crash WAL contention, or the vec shard mid-write — silently demotes to the hf-local
787:  * fallback even when ollama is healthy and reachable. createEmbeddingsProvider and getProvider use
788:  * this to prefer/recover ollama in that case. Returns false on any error (treat as unreachable).
789:  */
790: export async function isOllamaReachable(timeoutMs = 1500): Promise<boolean> {
791:   const baseUrl = (process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || 'http://127.0.0.1:11434').replace(/\/+$/, '');

796:     return response.ok;
797:   } catch {
798:     return false;
799:   } finally {

801:   }
802: }

861:     case 'ollama': {

878:         model,
879:         dim: options.dim,

882:         timeout: options.timeout,
883:       });
884:     }

886:     default:

891:   }

246: async function defaultHfLocalServerProbe(timeoutMs: number): Promise<HfLocalServerAvailability> {
247:   // hf-local is a pure-Node HTTP client; availability = the model
248:   // server endpoint answering /api/health (ready OR loading both count, since the
249:   // launcher lazily spawns + warms the server on first demand). No Python.
250:   return HfLocalProvider.canLoad({ timeout: timeoutMs });
251: }
252: 
253: async function delay(ms: number): Promise<void> {
254:   await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
255: }
256: 
257: async function acquireLock(
258:   lockPath: string,
259:   staleMs: number,
260:   sleepMs: number,
261: ): Promise<() => Promise<void>> {
262:   const deadline = Date.now() + staleMs;
263:   while (Date.now() < deadline) {
264:     try {
265:       await mkdir(lockPath, { recursive: false });
266:       await writeFile(
267:         path.join(lockPath, 'owner.json'),
268:         JSON.stringify({
269:           pid: process.pid,
270:           host: os.hostname(),
271:           acquiredAt: new Date().toISOString(),
272:         }, null, 2),
273:         'utf8',
274:       );
275:       return async () => {
276:         await rm(lockPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
277:       };
278:     } catch (error: unknown) {
279:       if ((error as { code?: string }).code !== 'EEXIST') {
280:         throw error;
281:       }
282:       try {
283:         const lockStat = await stat(lockPath);
284:         if (Date.now() - lockStat.mtimeMs > staleMs) {
285:           await rm(lockPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
286:           continue;
287:         }
288:       } catch {
289:         continue;
290:       }
291:       await delay(sleepMs);
292:     }
293:   }
294:   throw new Error(`Timed out waiting for embedder auto-selection lock: ${lockPath}`);
295: }
296: 
297: async function withOptionalLock<T>(
298:   options: AutoSelectOptions,
299:   operation: () => Promise<T>,
300: ): Promise<T> {
301:   if (!options.lockPath) {
302:     return operation();
303:   }
304: 
305:   const release = await acquireLock(
306:     options.lockPath,
307:     options.lockStaleMs ?? DEFAULT_LOCK_STALE_MS,
308:     options.sleepMs ?? DEFAULT_SLEEP_MS,
309:   );
310:   try {
311:     return await operation();
312:   } finally {
313:     await release();
314:   }
315: }

321: async function probeVoyage(context: ProbeContext): Promise<ProbeOutcome> {
322:   if (!hasUsableKey(context.env.VOYAGE_API_KEY)) {
323:     return { reason: 'VOYAGE_API_KEY not set or placeholder' };
324:   }
325: 
326:   try {
327:     const { response, body } = await fetchJsonWithTimeout(
328:       context.fetchImpl,
329:       `${resolveVoyageBaseUrl(context.env)}/embeddings`,
330:       {
331:         method: 'POST',
332:         headers: {
333:           'content-type': 'application/json',
334:           authorization: `Bearer ${context.env.VOYAGE_API_KEY}`,
335:         },
336:         body: JSON.stringify({
337:           input: ['spec-memory bootstrap probe'],
338:           model: VOYAGE_MODEL,
339:           input_type: 'query',
340:         }),
341:       },
342:       context.timeoutMs,
343:     );
344:     if (!response.ok) {
345:       return { reason: `Voyage embeddings probe returned ${response.status} ${response.statusText}` };
346:     }
347:     const dim = readEmbeddingDim(body) ?? VOYAGE_DIM;
348:     return {
349:       embedder: {
350:         name: VOYAGE_MODEL,
351:         dim,
352:         provider: 'voyage',
353:       },
354:       reason: `Voyage ${VOYAGE_MODEL} probe succeeded (${dim}d)`,
355:     };
356:   } catch (error: unknown) {
357:     return { reason: `Voyage embeddings probe failed: ${errorMessage(error)}` };
358:   }
359: }
360: 
361: async function probeOpenAi(context: ProbeContext): Promise<ProbeOutcome> {
362:   if (!hasUsableKey(context.env.OPENAI_API_KEY)) {
363:     return { reason: 'OPENAI_API_KEY not set or placeholder' };
364:   }
365: 
366:   try {
367:     const { response, body } = await fetchJsonWithTimeout(
368:       context.fetchImpl,
369:       `${resolveOpenAiBaseUrl(context.env)}/embeddings`,
370:       {
371:         method: 'POST',
372:         headers: {
373:           'content-type': 'application/json',
374:           authorization: `Bearer ${context.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}`,
375:         },
376:         body: JSON.stringify({
377:           input: 'spec-memory bootstrap probe',
378:           model: OPENAI_MODEL,
379:           encoding_format: 'float',
380:         }),
381:       },
382:       context.timeoutMs,
383:     );
384:     if (!response.ok) {
385:       return { reason: `OpenAI embeddings probe returned ${response.status} ${response.statusText}` };
386:     }
387:     const dim = readEmbeddingDim(body) ?? OPENAI_DIM;
388:     return {
389:       embedder: {
390:         name: OPENAI_MODEL,
391:         dim,
392:         provider: 'openai',
393:       },
394:       reason: `OpenAI ${OPENAI_MODEL} probe succeeded (${dim}d)`,
395:     };
396:   } catch (error: unknown) {
397:     return { reason: `OpenAI embeddings probe failed: ${errorMessage(error)}` };
398:   }
399: }
400: 
401: async function probeOllama(context: ProbeContext): Promise<ProbeOutcome> {
402:   const baseUrl = resolveOllamaBaseUrl(context.env);
403:   try {
404:     const { response, body } = await fetchJsonWithTimeout(
405:       context.fetchImpl,
406:       `${baseUrl}/api/tags`,
407:       { method: 'GET' },
408:       context.timeoutMs,
409:     );
410:     if (!response.ok) {
411:       return { reason: `Ollama /api/tags returned ${response.status} ${response.statusText}` };
412:     }
413:     const tags = parseOllamaTags(body);
414:     if (tags.size === 0) {
415:       return { reason: 'Ollama /api/tags returned no pulled models' };
416:     }
417:     for (const manifest of OLLAMA_PRIORITY) {
418:       if (tagsContain(tags, manifest.ollamaName) || tagsContain(tags, manifest.name)) {
419:         return {
420:           embedder: {
421:             name: manifest.name,
422:             dim: manifest.dim,
423:             provider: 'ollama',
424:           },
425:           reason: `Ollama has pulled model ${manifest.ollamaName}`,
426:         };
427:       }
428:     }
429:     return {
430:       reason: `Ollama reachable but none of ${OLLAMA_PRIORITY.map((manifest) => manifest.name).join(', ')} are pulled`,
431:     };
432:   } catch (error: unknown) {
433:     return { reason: `Ollama probe failed at ${baseUrl}: ${errorMessage(error)}` };
434:   }
435: }

437: async function probeHfLocal(context: ProbeContext): Promise<ProbeOutcome> {
438:   // Model name must resolve identically to HfLocalProvider, which only reads
439:   // HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL. Reading a separate HF_LOCAL_MODEL alias here would let
440:   // the cascade persist a model name the provider never uses.
441:   const model = context.env.HF_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL || HF_LOCAL_MODEL;
442:   const availability = await context.probeHfLocalServer(context.timeoutMs);
443:   if (!availability.available) {
444:     return { reason: availability.reason ?? 'hf-local model server is unreachable' };
445:   }
446:   // Only the canonical Nomic model has a known dimension at probe time. A custom
447:   // model's true dimension is unknown until its first embed, so persist 0 and let
448:   // HfLocalProvider's first-embed drift hook resolve and reconcile it — mirroring
449:   // the provider's own `model === DEFAULT_MODEL ? CANONICAL_DEFAULT_DIM : 0` rule.
450:   // Persisting a hardcoded 768 for a non-Nomic model would write a wrong dim.
451:   const dim = model === HF_LOCAL_MODEL ? HF_LOCAL_DIM : 0;
452:   return {
453:     embedder: {
454:       name: model,
455:       dim,
456:       provider: 'hf-local',
457:     },
458:     reason: `hf-local model server reachable via /api/health; selected ${model}`,
459:   };

1: // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Memory Index Discovery Helpers
3: // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: /* ------- 1. DEPENDENCIES ------- */
6: 
7: import fs from 'fs';
8: import path from 'path';
9: 
10: import { toErrorMessage } from '../utils/index.js';
11: import {
12:   extractSpecFolderFromGraphMetadataPath,
13:   GRAPH_METADATA_FILENAME,
14:   isGraphMetadataPath,
15:   matchesSpecDocumentPath,
16:   SPEC_DOCUMENT_FILENAMES,
17:   shouldDescendSpecDiscoveryDirectory,
18: } from '../lib/config/spec-doc-paths.js';

22: // Feature catalog: Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan)
23: // Feature catalog: Spec folder description discovery
24: 
25: 
26: /* ------- 2. CONSTANTS ------- */
27: 
28: const SPEC_DISCOVERY_MAX_DEPTH = 20;
29: const SPEC_DISCOVERY_MAX_NODES = 50_000;
30: 
31: interface DiscoveryWalkState {
32:   visitedNodes: number;
33:   maxNodesExceeded: boolean;
34:   depthExceeded: boolean;
35:   warnings: string[];
36: }
37: 
38: function shouldAbortDiscoveryWalk(state: DiscoveryWalkState, label: string): boolean {
39:   if (state.maxNodesExceeded) {
40:     return true;
41:   }
42: 
43:   state.visitedNodes += 1;
44:   if (state.visitedNodes > SPEC_DISCOVERY_MAX_NODES) {
45:     const warning = `[memory-index-discovery] ${label} aborted after ${SPEC_DISCOVERY_MAX_NODES} filesystem nodes`;
46:     console.warn(warning);
47:     state.warnings.push(warning);
48:     state.maxNodesExceeded = true;
49:     return true;
50:   }
51: 
52:   return false;
53: }
54: 
55: /* ------- 3. DISCOVERY FUNCTIONS ------- */
56: 
57: export interface SpecDiscoveryOptions {
58:   specFolder?: string | null;
59: }
60: 
61: export interface DiscoveryCapExceeded {
62:   maxNodes: boolean;
63:   depth: boolean;
64:   gitignoreSize: boolean;
65: }
66: 
67: export type DiscoveryFileList = string[] & {
68:   warnings: string[];
69:   capExceeded: DiscoveryCapExceeded;
70: };
71: 
72: function createDiscoveryState(): DiscoveryWalkState {
73:   return {
74:     visitedNodes: 0,
75:     maxNodesExceeded: false,
76:     depthExceeded: false,
77:     warnings: [],
78:   };
79: }
80: 
81: function attachDiscoveryMetadata(results: string[], state: DiscoveryWalkState): DiscoveryFileList {
82:   return Object.assign(results, {
83:     warnings: [...state.warnings],
84:     capExceeded: {
85:       maxNodes: state.maxNodesExceeded,
86:       depth: state.depthExceeded,
87:       gitignoreSize: false,
88:     },
89:   });
90: }
91: 
92: // Callers pass the scope in several natural shapes: the specs-root-relative
93: // form, the workspace path that still carries the configured specs-root marker,
94: // an absolute filesystem path, or a bare leaf folder name. Reduce all of them
95: // to the specs-root-relative form so the comparison against relativePath is
96: // stable regardless of which shape the caller used.
97: function normalizeSpecFolderScope(specFolder: string, specsRoot: string): string {
98:   let scope = specFolder.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '').replace(/^\.\//, '');
99:   if (path.isAbsolute(scope)) {
100:     const rel = path.relative(specsRoot, scope).replace(/\\/g, '/');
101:     return rel && !rel.startsWith('..') ? rel : scope;
102:   }
103:   const marker = '.opencode/specs/';
104:   const markerIdx = scope.indexOf(marker);
105:   if (markerIdx !== -1) {
106:     scope = scope.slice(markerIdx + marker.length);
107:   }
108:   return scope;
109: }
110: 
111: function matchesScopedSpecFolder(candidatePath: string, specsRoot: string, specFolder?: string | null): boolean {
112:   if (!specFolder) {
113:     return true;
114:   }
115: 
116:   const scope = normalizeSpecFolderScope(specFolder, specsRoot);
117:   const relativePath = path.relative(specsRoot, candidatePath).replace(/\\/g, '/');
118:   if (relativePath === scope || relativePath.startsWith(`${scope}/`)) {
119:     return true;
120:   }
121:   // A single-segment scope (a bare folder name) matches any file nested under a
122:   // directory of that name, preserving the documented leaf-name scoping form.
123:   if (scope.length > 0 && !scope.includes('/')) {
124:     const parentSegments = relativePath.split('/').slice(0, -1);
125:     return parentSegments.includes(scope);
126:   }
127:   return false;
128: }
129: 
130: /**
131:  * Discover spec folder documents in the configured specs directory tree.
132:  * Gated by SPEC_DOCUMENT_FILENAMES (see lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts) —
133:  * currently: spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, checklist.md, decision-record.md,
134:  * implementation-summary.md, research.md (incl. research/research.md),
135:  * handover.md, resource-map.md, review-report.md (review/), description.json
136:  * (incl. iteration packs' backfilled metadata under <pack>/iterations/).
137:  *
138:  * Excludes scratch/, memory/, and hidden directories; iteration dirs are
139:  * descended but only allowlisted filenames are collected.
140:  */
141: export function findSpecDocuments(workspacePath: string, options: SpecDiscoveryOptions = {}): DiscoveryFileList {
142:   // Respect explicit opt-out to disable spec document indexing.
143:   if (process.env.SPECKIT_INDEX_SPEC_DOCS === 'false') {
144:     return attachDiscoveryMetadata([], createDiscoveryState());
145:   }
146: 
147:   const results: string[] = [];
148:   const seenCanonicalRoots = new Set<string>();
149:   const seenCanonicalFiles = new Set<string>();
150: 
151:   const state = createDiscoveryState();
152: 
153:   function walkSpecsDir(specsRoot: string, dir: string, depth = 0): void {
154:     if (state.maxNodesExceeded) {
155:       return;

466: export class AutoSelectEmbedderError extends Error {
467:   readonly probes: readonly AutoSelectProbeResult[];
468: 
469:   constructor(probes: readonly AutoSelectProbeResult[]) {
470:     super(
471:       'Unable to auto-select an active embedder. Probed tiers: '
472:       + probes.map((probe) => `${probe.tier}: ${probe.reason}`).join('; '),
473:     );
474:     this.name = 'AutoSelectEmbedderError';
475:     this.probes = probes;
476:   }
477: }
478: 
479: async function selectWithoutPersistence(options: AutoSelectOptions): Promise<AutoSelectResult> {
480:   const context: ProbeContext = {
481:     env: options.env ?? process.env,
482:     fetchImpl: options.fetchImpl ?? fetch,
483:     timeoutMs: options.timeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS,
484:     probeHfLocalServer: options.probeHfLocalServer ?? defaultHfLocalServerProbe,
485:   };
486: 
487:   const probes: AutoSelectProbeResult[] = [];
488:   // Local-first cascade. Try Ollama first, fall through to hf-local
489:   // (pure-Node @huggingface/transformers HTTP model server), then escalate to
490:   // cloud APIs (OpenAI, Voyage) only when nothing local works.
491:   const sequence: Array<readonly [AutoSelectedEmbedderProvider, (ctx: ProbeContext) => Promise<ProbeOutcome>]> = [
492:     ['ollama', probeOllama],
493:     ['hf-local', probeHfLocal],
494:     ['openai', probeOpenAi],
495:     ['voyage', probeVoyage],
496:   ];
497: 
498:   // Honor an explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER ahead of the local-first cascade: an
499:   // operator who pins a provider should have it tried first, then fall back to
500:   // the cascade if it is unreachable. Mirrors resolveProvider() precedence.
501:   const explicitProvider = context.env.EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER?.trim().toLowerCase();
502:   if (explicitProvider && explicitProvider !== 'auto') {
503:     const idx = sequence.findIndex(([tier]) => tier === explicitProvider);
504:     if (idx > 0) {
505:       const [preferred] = sequence.splice(idx, 1);
506:       sequence.unshift(preferred);
507:     }
508:   }
509: 
510:   for (const [tier, probe] of sequence) {
511:     const outcome = await probe(context);
512:     probes.push({
513:       tier,
514:       ok: Boolean(outcome.embedder),
515:       reason: outcome.reason,
516:     });
517:     if (outcome.embedder) {
518:       return {
519:         ...outcome.embedder,
520:         probes,
521:       };
522:     }
523:   }
524: 
525:   throw new AutoSelectEmbedderError(probes);
526: }
527: 
528: export async function autoSelectActiveEmbedder(options: AutoSelectOptions = {}): Promise<AutoSelectResult> {
529:   return withOptionalLock(options, async () => {
530:     const existing = options.metadataStore?.readActiveEmbedder();
531:     if (existing) {
532:       return {
533:         ...existing,
534:         probes: [{
535:           tier: existing.provider,
536:           ok: true,
537:           reason: 'active embedder already persisted in vec_metadata',
538:         }],
539:       };
540:     }
541: 
542:     const selected = await selectWithoutPersistence(options);
543:     options.metadataStore?.persistActiveEmbedder(selected);
544:     return selected;
545:   });
546: }
547: 
548: export function providerResolutionFromAutoSelect(result: AutoSelectedEmbedder): ProviderResolution {
549:   return {
550:     name: result.provider,
551:     reason: `auto-selected ${result.name} from bootstrap precedence chain`,
552:   };
553: }

The socket-based `od` CLI is stable across restarts, only the HTTP port moves. Full HTTP detail: [`references/od_cli_reference.md`](references/od_cli_reference.md).

The canonical source for this entry is `GET /api/mcp/install-info`, where `command[0]` is the "Open Design Helper" Electron binary, `args` is `[<daemon-cli.mjs>, "mcp"]`, and `daemonUrl` is the live HTTP base. The MCP server re-discovers the live ephemeral daemon URL from the socket on each spawn, so the config stays valid across daemon restarts. Run `--print --json` first and read the exact `command`/`env` it will write. Full detail + manual config: [`references/mcp_wiring.md`](references/mcp_wiring.md).

  Line 93: `od mcp install <agent>` registers Open Design's stdio MCP server into a supported terminal agent. Supported agent slugs include `claude`, `codex`, `cursor`, `copilot`, `openclaw`, `antigravity`, `gemini`, `pi`, `vibe`, `hermes`, `cline`, `kimi`, `trae`, and `opencode`. For opencode it deep-merges `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` under `mcp.open-design`; for Claude Code it delegates to `claude mcp add --scope user open-design`. The dry-run form `--print --json` writes nothing and prints the exact entry, so the operator reviews the command array and environment before anything lands. The written entry re-discovers the live daemon URL from the socket on each spawn, so it survives daemon restarts. The skill never pipes a remote install script to a shell.

52: function loadEnvFile(filePath) {
53:   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return 0;

55:   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');

59:     const m = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/);

61:     const key = m[1];
62:     let val = m[2];
63:     // Strip matching outer quotes (single or double)
64:     if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
65:       val = val.slice(1, -1);

119: // single-writer lock". Never counts toward the crash loop: the right move is
120: // bridging to the live owner (or a bounded retry while a maintenance script
121: // finishes), not relaunching a doomed second writer. Mirrored in
122: // mcp_server context-server (EXIT_DB_LOCK_HELD).
123: const EXIT_DB_LOCK_HELD = 86;

128:   process.stderr.write(`[mk-spec-memory-launcher] ${message}\n`);
129:   persistLauncherLogLine(`${new Date().toISOString()} [pid ${process.pid}] ${message}\n`);
130: }

132: function isDurableWriteUnavailable(error) {
133:   const code = error && typeof error === 'object' ? error.code : undefined;
134:   return DURABLE_WRITE_UNAVAILABLE_CODES.has(code);
135: }
136: 
137: function logDurableWriteUnavailableOnce(operation, targetPath, error) {
138:   const code = error && typeof error === 'object' ? error.code : 'UNKNOWN';
139:   const key = `${operation}:${targetPath}:${code}`;
140:   if (durableWriteWarnings.has(key)) return;
141:   durableWriteWarnings.add(key);
142:   const detail = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error ?? code);
143:   log(`${operation} skipped for ${rel(targetPath)}: ${code} ${detail}`);
144: }

146: // Persistent launcher log: log() writes to stderr, which the MCP host captures inconsistently; it
147: // ALSO appends here so an owner-disposal race or daemon flap is attributable after the fact from a
148: // durable file. The append is best-effort and size-bounded — a logging failure must never affect the
149: // launcher, and the file rotates to a single previous generation once it crosses the cap so it cannot
150: // grow without bound.
151: function launcherLogIsEnabled(env = process.env) {
152:   return env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_LOG !== '0';
153: }
154: function launcherLogMaxBytes(env = process.env) {
155:   return parsePositiveInteger(env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_LOG_MAX_BYTES, 1024 * 1024);
156: }
157: function resolveLauncherLogPath(env = process.env, baseDir = dbDir) {
158:   const override = env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_LOG_PATH;
159:   return typeof override === 'string' && override.trim()
160:     ? override.trim()
161:     : path.join(baseDir, '.mk-spec-memory-launcher.log');
162: }
163: function shouldRotateLauncherLog(currentSizeBytes, maxBytes) {
164:   return Number.isFinite(currentSizeBytes) && Number.isFinite(maxBytes) && currentSizeBytes > maxBytes;
165: }
166: function persistLauncherLogLine(line) {
167:   if (!launcherLogIsEnabled()) return;
168:   let target;
169:   try {
170:     target = resolveLauncherLogPath();
171:     try {
172:       const { size } = fs.statSync(target);
173:       if (shouldRotateLauncherLog(size, launcherLogMaxBytes())) {
174:         try {
175:           // Rotate to a DISTINCT sibling. A `.log` path keeps a `.prev.log` (still matched by the
176:           // *.log gitignore); any other operator-overridden path appends `.prev` so a non-`.log`
177:           // suffix still rotates instead of renaming the file onto itself and growing without bound.
178:           const rotated = target.endsWith('.log') ? target.replace(/\.log$/, '.prev.log') : `${target}.prev`;
179:           fs.renameSync(target, rotated);
180:         } catch {
181:           // Best-effort rotation: if the rename fails, keep appending to the current file.
182:         }
183:       }
184:     } catch (statErr) {
185:       if (statErr.code !== 'ENOENT') throw statErr;
186:     }
187:     fs.appendFileSync(target, line, { mode: 0o600 });
188:   } catch (error) {
189:     if (target && isDurableWriteUnavailable(error)) {
190:       logDurableWriteUnavailableOnce('launcher-log-append', target, error);
191:       return;
192:     }
193:     // Logging must never break the launcher.
194:   }
195: }

197: // Daemon re-election (on by default; set SPECKIT_DAEMON_REELECTION=0 or off to restore kill-on-disposal).
198: // When enabled the owner spawns the daemon detached and, on its own shutdown, RELEASES the daemon
199: // (leaves it running for a live secondary to bridge to) instead of killing it, so a session ending does
200: // not tear the shared backend out from under other sessions. A fresh session that finds the released
201: // daemon under a stale lease ADOPTS it through the bridge when the recorded child is alive and bridgeable;
202: // it reaps and respawns only when that daemon is dead or unbridgeable, so the database keeps a single
203: // writer either way. Disabling collapses every path below to the prior behavior (daemon tied to the owner,

1: ---
2: title: Hook System Reference
3: description: Current hook registration, runtime vocabulary, lifecycle behavior, and fallback contracts for Spec Kit prompt-time and session hooks.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "hook system reference"
6:   - "hook registration matrix"

8:   - "hook lifecycle behavior"
9:   - "codex timeout fallback"
10: importance_tier: important

12: ---
13: 
14: # Hook System Reference
15: 
16: Current hook registration, runtime vocabulary, lifecycle behavior, and fallback contracts for Spec Kit prompt-time and session hooks.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 1. OVERVIEW
21: 
22: The hook system provides automated context preservation at hook-capable prompt-time and lifecycle boundaries. Prompt delivery, startup wiring, compaction, and shutdown handling differ by runtime, but they call the same retrieval primitives and fail open to the same operator recovery path.
23: 
24: ---
25: 
26: ## 2. HOOK REGISTRATION
27: 
28: Claude Code hooks are registered in `.claude/settings.local.json`. Under the normalized Claude schema, `UserPromptSubmit` is the prompt hook, while `PreCompact`, `SessionStart`, and `Stop` are lifecycle hooks:
29: 
30: ```json
31: {
32:   "hooks": {
33:     "UserPromptSubmit": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
34:     "PreCompact": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/compact-inject.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
35:     "SessionStart": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/session-prime.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
36:     "Stop": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/session-stop.js", "async": true, "timeout": 10 }] }]
37:   }
38: }
39: ```
40: 
41: Copilot registration is owned by the Copilot-local README at `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md`. Copilot does not use the Claude Code `.claude/settings.local.json` nested hook block and must not add top-level `type`, `bash`, or `timeoutSec` wrapper fields there. The current Copilot path runs Copilot-supported writer scripts, including the checked-in `.github/hooks/superset-notify.json` wrapper where available, and refreshes the managed custom-instructions block for the next prompt. Copilot advisor freshness is NEXT-PROMPT: the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.
42: 
43: Codex registration is owned by the user/workspace Codex runtime config, not by the repo template alone. The checked-in `.codex/settings.json` is an example hook template for the project commands. Live native Codex readiness requires `[features].codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml` or an equivalent `--enable codex_hooks` launch flag, plus `~/.codex/hooks.json` or workspace `hooks.json` registration for the compiled Spec Kit hooks.
44: 
45: ### Codex Hook Contract Versions
46: 
47: | Contract | Date / version note | Registration point | Trigger | Status |
48: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
49: | Legacy repo template | Retained for repo examples and local policy shape | `.codex/settings.json` | None by itself; copy/adapt into live Codex hook registration | Example only |
50: | Current native hook contract | Active runtime contract as of 2026-04-29 | `~/.codex/config.toml` with `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus `~/.codex/hooks.json` or workspace `hooks.json` | Codex `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, and optional `PreToolUse` native hooks | Authoritative live registration |
51: | Fallback prompt wrapper | Compatibility behavior | `hooks/codex/prompt-wrapper.ts` when native hook readiness reports unavailable | Explicit wrapper invocation | Fallback, not native hook registration |
52: 
53: ---
54: 
55: ## 3. CANONICAL RUNTIME HOOK VOCABULARY
56: 
57: Use support names first, then map to the runtime-local surface below when wiring or validating a specific runtime:
58: 
59: | Support | Claude / Codex / Copilot | OpenCode | Trigger / fallback |
60: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
61: | Prompt-time advisor | `UserPromptSubmit` | `experimental.chat.system.transform` | Runtime prompt hook; Copilot is NEXT-PROMPT freshness, so current prompt sees PRIOR turn's brief; fallback is explicit `skill_advisor.py` or advisor MCP tooling |
62: | Session priming | `SessionStart` | `event` startup handlers | Runtime startup hook; fallback is `/speckit:resume` or `session_bootstrap()` |
63: | Compaction | `PreCompact` | `event` compact handlers / compact plugin | Runtime compaction event; fallback is resume ladder |
64: | Session cleanup | `Stop` | `event` cleanup handlers | Runtime session-end event where supported; fallback is `/memory:save` |
65: 
66: ---
67: 
68: ## 4. HOOK LIFECYCLE
69: 
70: 1. **Prompt-time advisor** — `UserPromptSubmit` in Claude, Codex, and Copilot; `experimental.chat.system.transform` in OpenCode. Claude, Codex, and OpenCode can inject runtime-visible advisor context in-turn. Copilot uses the same logical surface to refresh managed custom instructions and returns `{}`; this is NEXT-PROMPT freshness, so the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.
71: 2. **Compaction** — `PreCompact` in Claude, compact `event` handlers in OpenCode, and limited wrapper-only parity in Copilot. Stdout is not injected on the precompute phase.
72: 3. **SessionStart** — Fires on session start. Routes by source:
73:    - `compact`: Reads cached PreCompact payload, injects via stdout
74:    - `startup`: Primes with Spec Kit Memory overview
75:    - `resume`: Loads prior session state — respects the phase-parent pointer redirect documented in `references/hooks/skill_advisor_hook.md`. When the resume target is a phase parent and `derived.last_active_child_id` is fresh (<24h), priming surfaces the active child rather than the parent's listing
76:    - `clear`: Minimal output
77:    Copilot keeps runtime-specific transport output, but can forward the same session and spec-folder startup scope used by Claude when that input is available. Codex only reports live native-hook readiness when `[features].codex_hooks = true` is enabled in `~/.codex/config.toml` or equivalent launch flags and a user/workspace `hooks.json` is wired. Repo `.codex/settings.json` is a template/example, not the live readiness predicate.
78: 4. **Session cleanup** — `Stop` in Claude and cleanup `event` handlers in OpenCode. Parses transcript JSONL for token usage, calculates cost estimates, and stores snapshots when the runtime supports it.
79: 
80: ---
81: 
82: ## 5. HOOK STATE
83: 
84: Per-session state stored at `${os.tmpdir()}/speckit-claude-hooks/<project-hash>/<session-id>.json`.
85: 
86: Fields: `claudeSessionId`, `speckitSessionId`, `lastSpecFolder`, `pendingCompactPrime`, `metrics` (prompt/completion tokens, transcript offset).
87: 
88: ---
89: 
90: ## 6. SCRIPT LOCATIONS
91: 
92: Source: `mcp_server/hooks/claude/*.ts`
93: Compiled: `mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/*.js`
94: 
95: ---
96: 
97: ## 7. TOKEN BUDGETS
98: 
99: - Compaction injection: 4000 tokens (`COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET`)
100: - Session priming: 2000 tokens (`SESSION_PRIME_TOKEN_BUDGET`)
101: - Hook timeout: 1800ms (`HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS`, under 2s hard cap)
102: 
103: ---
104: 
105: ## 8. RUNTIME HOOK MATRIX
106: 
107: Prompt hooks and lifecycle hooks are separate support. A runtime can support prompt-time advisor context without supporting startup/code-graph lifecycle injection, and Copilot's prompt-time parity is NEXT-PROMPT freshness rather than in-turn injection; the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.
108: 
109: | Runtime | Prompt hook | Lifecycle hook | Compaction | Stop | Trigger / default | Manual fallback |
110: | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
111: | Claude | yes (`UserPromptSubmit`) | yes (`SessionStart`) | yes (`PreCompact`) | yes (`Stop`) | `.claude/settings.local.json` hook events | `/speckit:resume`, `/memory:save`, direct MCP tools |
112: | Codex | yes (`UserPromptSubmit`) | yes (`SessionStart`, live only when `codex_hooks` and `hooks.json` are both present) | no | no Spec Kit cleanup hook | `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus user/workspace `hooks.json`; `.codex/settings.json` is template-only | `/speckit:resume`, `session_bootstrap()`, prompt-wrapper fallback |
113: | Copilot | yes (file-based custom instructions; NEXT-PROMPT freshness; current prompt sees PRIOR turn's brief) | yes (`SessionStart` writer) | limited cache/writer path; no model-visible precompute injection | n/a | Copilot-supported writer scripts; see `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md` | Managed instructions file or `/speckit:resume` |
114: | OpenCode | yes (`experimental.chat.system.transform`) | yes (`event` startup handlers) | yes (`event` compact handlers / compact plugin) | yes (`event` cleanup handlers) | Plugin bridge and event handlers | `/speckit:resume`, direct MCP tools |
115: 
116: ### Codex Timeout Fallback Semantics
117: 
118: Codex `UserPromptSubmit` uses `SPECKIT_CODEX_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS` (default `3000`) for the advisor builder. If the builder times out, the hook returns prompt-safe stale context with a machine-visible marker: `stale: true`, `reason: "timeout-fallback"`. The hook also writes one structured warning line (`event: "codex_user_prompt_timeout_fallback"`) before its normal prompt-safe diagnostic. Operators can run the smoke helper in `hooks/codex/lib/freshness-smoke-check.ts` to check cold-start freshness (`fresh`, `lastUpdateAt`, `latencyMs`) before adjusting timeout values.
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## 9. CROSS-RUNTIME FALLBACK
123: 
124: Claude Code uses native `UserPromptSubmit`, `SessionStart`, `PreCompact`, and `Stop` hooks. Copilot CLI uses Copilot-supported writer scripts that refresh the Spec Kit managed block in `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md`; hook output remains `{}` with NEXT-PROMPT freshness semantics, so the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief, and the registration contract is documented in `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md`. Do not use the stale merged `.claude/settings.local.json` wrapper shape for Copilot. OpenCode uses plugin-based transport rather than shell wrappers: `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js` delivers prompt-time advisor briefs through `experimental.chat.system.transform`, `.opencode/plugins/mk-spec-memory.js` bridges Spec Kit Memory tools through the warm daemon-backed spec-memory CLI, while `.opencode/plugins/mk-code-graph.js` (CLI-backed via its plugin bridge) and plugin `event` handlers cover startup, compaction, readiness, and session cleanup. Codex CLI only reports live native-hook readiness when `[features].codex_hooks = true` is enabled in `~/.codex/config.toml` or equivalent launch flags and user/workspace `hooks.json` is wired; on `UserPromptSubmit` timeout, Codex returns a stale fallback marker (`stale:true`, `reason:"timeout-fallback"`) and logs a structured warning instead of silently serving cold-start context. Use `/speckit:resume` when hooks are unavailable or disabled. If automatic hook delivery is unavailable in any runtime, or the advisor hook path is intentionally disabled (`SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1`), fall back to the canonical operator path: start with `/speckit:resume`, rebuild packet continuity from `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`, then use `session_bootstrap()` or `session_resume()` only when you need lower-level structural health or merged recovery detail.
125: 
126: MCP remains the registered primary transport for all three daemons; the daemon-backed CLI shims (`.opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/code-index.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/skill-advisor.cjs`) are an additive dual-stack fallback over the same warm daemons. When a runtime's MCP tools are missing, fail to initialize, or return transport errors while the daemon is otherwise expected to be warm, hooks and operators route through the CLI in warm-only mode: probe the daemon IPC socket first and skip if absent — prompt-time paths must never cold-spawn a daemon — and treat exit `75` as retryable daemon/IPC unavailability rather than user error.
127: 
128: ---
129: 
130: ## 10. SHARED STARTUP PAYLOAD PARITY
131: 
132: All four supported runtimes transport the same compact startup shared-payload through their runtime-specific hooks. The payload is produced by `buildStartupBrief()`, which is defined locally in each runtime's session-prime hook and delegates to `getStartupBriefFromMarker()` in `mcp_server/lib/code-graph-boundary.ts`; it includes `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment summary) alongside the `sharedPayloadTransport` envelope. Runtime-specific startup entrypoints:
133: 
134: | Runtime | Startup entrypoint | Transport | Trigger |
135: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
136: | Claude | `hooks/claude/session-prime.ts` | stdout context injection | Claude `SessionStart` |
137: | Copilot | `hooks/copilot/session-prime.ts` | refreshes managed block in `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md` | Copilot-supported writer script; next prompt observes the block |
138: | Codex | `hooks/codex/session-start.ts` | native `SessionStart` hook | `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus user/workspace `hooks.json` |
139: 
140: `graphQualitySummary` is also surfaced on `code_graph_status` and `session_bootstrap()` / startup-brief responses, so operators can inspect the same quality envelope regardless of transport. `session_bootstrap()` remains a manual fallback when native startup hooks are disabled — it is no longer a Codex-only substitute.
141: 
142: Hooks remain in `system-spec-kit` because they own runtime lifecycle transport. Code-graph readiness, quality, and package docs are owned by the sibling `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/` skill and imported through the co-resident MCP registration path.
143: 
144: ---
145: 
146: ## 11. ADVISOR BRIDGE AND THRESHOLD CONTRACT
147: 
148: OpenCode delivers prompt-time advisor context through a plugin-helper bridge rather than a shell wrapper:
149: 
150: - Plugin: `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js`
151: - Bridge entrypoint: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs`
152: - Target: native compat entrypoint under `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/compat/`
153: 
154: The bridge routes through the shared `renderAdvisorBrief(...)` invariants used by the Codex prompt-submit hook and prompt-wrapper fallback. The default OpenCode prompt-time threshold contract is **`0.8` confidence / `0.35` uncertainty**.
155: 
156: The public MCP contract is unified across runtime hook surfaces:
157: 
158: - `advisor_recommend` and `advisor_validate` accept an explicit `workspaceRoot` argument; both responses surface the resolved `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds` used for routing.
159: - `advisor_status` is diagnostic-only and does not rebuild. Use `advisor_rebuild({})` when status reports stale/absent/unavailable, or `advisor_rebuild({"force":true})` for an explicit live-state rebuild.
160: - `advisor_validate` additionally publishes `thresholdSemantics` (aggregate vs runtime) plus a prompt-safe `telemetry.outcomes.totals` block (`accepted` / `corrected` / `ignored`).
161: - Hook diagnostics persist to bounded JSONL sinks under the temp metrics root (`${TMPDIR}/speckit-advisor-metrics/`), so `advisor_validate` can read the sinks back across processes.
162: 
163: ---
164: 
165: ## 12. RETRIEVAL PRIMITIVES
166: 
167: The same retrieval building blocks power both hook delivery and explicit recovery:
168: 1. `memory_match_triggers(prompt)` — Fast turn-start context
169: 2. `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` — Continuation and compaction recovery core
170: 3. `session_bootstrap()` / `session_resume()` — Session-oriented wrappers that layer health and structural context around resume retrieval
171: 
172: ---
173: 
174: ## 13. READ-ONLY GOVERNANCE DIAGNOSTICS
175: 
176: Two passive governance checks fire from existing surfaces and do not change recall or write stored memory data:
177: 
178: - `memory_health` includes `data.exclusionAudit`, derived from the hard-exclusion predicates exposed by `hybrid-search.ts`. `/doctor memory` reads that block through the existing health call and raises `hard_exclusion_risk` when deprecated-tier exclusions are risky or when an exclusion source is unclassified.
179: - The tool-ownership map is generated from `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` and checked by the project pre-commit hook. Drift between `mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json` and the live definitions blocks commits; the map and this document are outputs, never inputs to the ownership source of truth.
180:

# Hook System Reference

Current hook registration, runtime vocabulary, lifecycle behavior, and fallback contracts for Spec Kit prompt-time and session hooks.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

The hook system provides automated context preservation at hook-capable prompt-time and lifecycle boundaries. Prompt delivery, startup wiring, compaction, and shutdown handling differ by runtime, but they call the same retrieval primitives and fail open to the same operator recovery path.

---

## 2. HOOK REGISTRATION

Claude Code hooks are registered in `.claude/settings.local.json`. Under the normalized Claude schema, `UserPromptSubmit` is the prompt hook, while `PreCompact`, `SessionStart`, and `Stop` are lifecycle hooks:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "UserPromptSubmit": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
    "PreCompact": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/compact-inject.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
    "SessionStart": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/session-prime.js", "timeout": 3 }] }],
    "Stop": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/session-stop.js", "async": true, "timeout": 10 }] }]
  }
}
```

Copilot registration is owned by the Copilot-local README at `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md`. Copilot does not use the Claude Code `.claude/settings.local.json` nested hook block and must not add top-level `type`, `bash`, or `timeoutSec` wrapper fields there. The current Copilot path runs Copilot-supported writer scripts, including the checked-in `.github/hooks/superset-notify.json` wrapper where available, and refreshes the managed custom-instructions block for the next prompt. Copilot advisor freshness is NEXT-PROMPT: the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.

Codex registration is owned by the user/workspace Codex runtime config, not by the repo template alone. The checked-in `.codex/settings.json` is an example hook template for the project commands. Live native Codex readiness requires `[features].codex_hooks = true` in `~/.codex/config.toml` or an equivalent `--enable codex_hooks` launch flag, plus `~/.codex/hooks.json` or workspace `hooks.json` registration for the compiled Spec Kit hooks.

### Codex Hook Contract Versions

| Contract | Date / version note | Registration point | Trigger | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Legacy repo template | Retained for repo examples and local policy shape | `.codex/settings.json` | None by itself; copy/adapt into live Codex hook registration | Example only |
| Current native hook contract | Active runtime contract as of 2026-04-29 | `~/.codex/config.toml` with `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus `~/.codex/hooks.json` or workspace `hooks.json` | Codex `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, and optional `PreToolUse` native hooks | Authoritative live registration |
| Fallback prompt wrapper | Compatibility behavior | `hooks/codex/prompt-wrapper.ts` when native hook readiness reports unavailable | Explicit wrapper invocation | Fallback, not native hook registration |

---

## 3. CANONICAL RUNTIME HOOK VOCABULARY

Use support names first, then map to the runtime-local surface below when wiring or validating a specific runtime:

| Support | Claude / Codex / Copilot | OpenCode | Trigger / fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Prompt-time advisor | `UserPromptSubmit` | `experimental.chat.system.transform` | Runtime prompt hook; Copilot is NEXT-PROMPT freshness, so current prompt sees PRIOR turn's brief; fallback is explicit `skill_advisor.py` or advisor MCP tooling |
| Session priming | `SessionStart` | `event` startup handlers | Runtime startup hook; fallback is `/speckit:resume` or `session_bootstrap()` |
| Compaction | `PreCompact` | `event` compact handlers / compact plugin | Runtime compaction event; fallback is resume ladder |
| Session cleanup | `Stop` | `event` cleanup handlers | Runtime session-end event where supported; fallback is `/memory:save` |

---

## 4. HOOK LIFECYCLE

1. **Prompt-time advisor** — `UserPromptSubmit` in Claude, Codex, and Copilot; `experimental.chat.system.transform` in OpenCode. Claude, Codex, and OpenCode can inject runtime-visible advisor context in-turn. Copilot uses the same logical surface to refresh managed custom instructions and returns `{}`; this is NEXT-PROMPT freshness, so the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.
2. **Compaction** — `PreCompact` in Claude, compact `event` handlers in OpenCode, and limited wrapper-only parity in Copilot. Stdout is not injected on the precompute phase.
3. **SessionStart** — Fires on session start. Routes by source:
   - `compact`: Reads cached PreCompact payload, injects via stdout
   - `startup`: Primes with Spec Kit Memory overview
   - `resume`: Loads prior session state — respects the phase-parent pointer redirect documented in `references/hooks/skill_advisor_hook.md`. When the resume target is a phase parent and `derived.last_active_child_id` is fresh (<24h), priming surfaces the active child rather than the parent's listing
   - `clear`: Minimal output
   Copilot keeps runtime-specific transport output, but can forward the same session and spec-folder startup scope used by Claude when that input is available. Codex only reports live native-hook readiness when `[features].codex_hooks = true` is enabled in `~/.codex/config.toml` or equivalent launch flags and a user/workspace `hooks.json` is wired. Repo `.codex/settings.json` is a template/example, not the live readiness predicate.
4. **Session cleanup** — `Stop` in Claude and cleanup `event` handlers in OpenCode. Parses transcript JSONL for token usage, calculates cost estimates, and stores snapshots when the runtime supports it.

---

## 5. HOOK STATE

Per-session state stored at `${os.tmpdir()}/speckit-claude-hooks/<project-hash>/<session-id>.json`.

Fields: `claudeSessionId`, `speckitSessionId`, `lastSpecFolder`, `pendingCompactPrime`, `metrics` (prompt/completion tokens, transcript offset).

---

## 6. SCRIPT LOCATIONS

Source: `mcp_server/hooks/claude/*.ts`
Compiled: `mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/*.js`

---

## 7. TOKEN BUDGETS

- Compaction injection: 4000 tokens (`COMPACTION_TOKEN_BUDGET`)
- Session priming: 2000 tokens (`SESSION_PRIME_TOKEN_BUDGET`)
- Hook timeout: 1800ms (`HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS`, under 2s hard cap)

---

## 8. RUNTIME HOOK MATRIX

Prompt hooks and lifecycle hooks are separate support. A runtime can support prompt-time advisor context without supporting startup/code-graph lifecycle injection, and Copilot's prompt-time parity is NEXT-PROMPT freshness rather than in-turn injection; the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief.

| Runtime | Prompt hook | Lifecycle hook | Compaction | Stop | Trigger / default | Manual fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Claude | yes (`UserPromptSubmit`) | yes (`SessionStart`) | yes (`PreCompact`) | yes (`Stop`) | `.claude/settings.local.json` hook events | `/speckit:resume`, `/memory:save`, direct MCP tools |
| Codex | yes (`UserPromptSubmit`) | yes (`SessionStart`, live only when `codex_hooks` and `hooks.json` are both present) | no | no Spec Kit cleanup hook | `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus user/workspace `hooks.json`; `.codex/settings.json` is template-only | `/speckit:resume`, `session_bootstrap()`, prompt-wrapper fallback |
| Copilot | yes (file-based custom instructions; NEXT-PROMPT freshness; current prompt sees PRIOR turn's brief) | yes (`SessionStart` writer) | limited cache/writer path; no model-visible precompute injection | n/a | Copilot-supported writer scripts; see `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md` | Managed instructions file or `/speckit:resume` |
| OpenCode | yes (`experimental.chat.system.transform`) | yes (`event` startup handlers) | yes (`event` compact handlers / compact plugin) | yes (`event` cleanup handlers) | Plugin bridge and event handlers | `/speckit:resume`, direct MCP tools |

### Codex Timeout Fallback Semantics

Codex `UserPromptSubmit` uses `SPECKIT_CODEX_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS` (default `3000`) for the advisor builder. If the builder times out, the hook returns prompt-safe stale context with a machine-visible marker: `stale: true`, `reason: "timeout-fallback"`. The hook also writes one structured warning line (`event: "codex_user_prompt_timeout_fallback"`) before its normal prompt-safe diagnostic. Operators can run the smoke helper in `hooks/codex/lib/freshness-smoke-check.ts` to check cold-start freshness (`fresh`, `lastUpdateAt`, `latencyMs`) before adjusting timeout values.

---

## 9. CROSS-RUNTIME FALLBACK

Claude Code uses native `UserPromptSubmit`, `SessionStart`, `PreCompact`, and `Stop` hooks. Copilot CLI uses Copilot-supported writer scripts that refresh the Spec Kit managed block in `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md`; hook output remains `{}` with NEXT-PROMPT freshness semantics, so the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief, and the registration contract is documented in `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md`. Do not use the stale merged `.claude/settings.local.json` wrapper shape for Copilot. OpenCode uses plugin-based transport rather than shell wrappers: `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js` delivers prompt-time advisor briefs through `experimental.chat.system.transform`, `.opencode/plugins/mk-spec-memory.js` bridges Spec Kit Memory tools through the warm daemon-backed spec-memory CLI, while `.opencode/plugins/mk-code-graph.js` (CLI-backed via its plugin bridge) and plugin `event` handlers cover startup, compaction, readiness, and session cleanup. Codex CLI only reports live native-hook readiness when `[features].codex_hooks = true` is enabled in `~/.codex/config.toml` or equivalent launch flags and user/workspace `hooks.json` is wired; on `UserPromptSubmit` timeout, Codex returns a stale fallback marker (`stale:true`, `reason:"timeout-fallback"`) and logs a structured warning instead of silently serving cold-start context. Use `/speckit:resume` when hooks are unavailable or disabled. If automatic hook delivery is unavailable in any runtime, or the advisor hook path is intentionally disabled (`SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1`), fall back to the canonical operator path: start with `/speckit:resume`, rebuild packet continuity from `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`, then use `session_bootstrap()` or `session_resume()` only when you need lower-level structural health or merged recovery detail.

MCP remains the registered primary transport for all three daemons; the daemon-backed CLI shims (`.opencode/bin/spec-memory.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/code-index.cjs`, `.opencode/bin/skill-advisor.cjs`) are an additive dual-stack fallback over the same warm daemons. When a runtime's MCP tools are missing, fail to initialize, or return transport errors while the daemon is otherwise expected to be warm, hooks and operators route through the CLI in warm-only mode: probe the daemon IPC socket first and skip if absent — prompt-time paths must never cold-spawn a daemon — and treat exit `75` as retryable daemon/IPC unavailability rather than user error.

---

## 10. SHARED STARTUP PAYLOAD PARITY

All four supported runtimes transport the same compact startup shared-payload through their runtime-specific hooks. The payload is produced by `buildStartupBrief()`, which is defined locally in each runtime's session-prime hook and delegates to `getStartupBriefFromMarker()` in `mcp_server/lib/code-graph-boundary.ts`; it includes `graphQualitySummary` (detector provenance + edge-enrichment summary) alongside the `sharedPayloadTransport` envelope. Runtime-specific startup entrypoints:

| Runtime | Startup entrypoint | Transport | Trigger |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Claude | `hooks/claude/session-prime.ts` | stdout context injection | Claude `SessionStart` |
| Copilot | `hooks/copilot/session-prime.ts` | refreshes managed block in `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md` | Copilot-supported writer script; next prompt observes the block |
| Codex | `hooks/codex/session-start.ts` | native `SessionStart` hook | `[features].codex_hooks = true` plus user/workspace `hooks.json` |

`graphQualitySummary` is also surfaced on `code_graph_status` and `session_bootstrap()` / startup-brief responses, so operators can inspect the same quality envelope regardless of transport. `session_bootstrap()` remains a manual fallback when native startup hooks are disabled — it is no longer a Codex-only substitute.

Hooks remain in `system-spec-kit` because they own runtime lifecycle transport. Code-graph readiness, quality, and package docs are owned by the sibling `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/` skill and imported through the co-resident MCP registration path.

---

## 11. ADVISOR BRIDGE AND THRESHOLD CONTRACT

OpenCode delivers prompt-time advisor context through a plugin-helper bridge rather than a shell wrapper:

- Plugin: `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js`
- Bridge entrypoint: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs`
- Target: native compat entrypoint under `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/compat/`

The bridge routes through the shared `renderAdvisorBrief(...)` invariants used by the Codex prompt-submit hook and prompt-wrapper fallback. The default OpenCode prompt-time threshold contract is **`0.8` confidence / `0.35` uncertainty**.

The public MCP contract is unified across runtime hook surfaces:

- `advisor_recommend` and `advisor_validate` accept an explicit `workspaceRoot` argument; both responses surface the resolved `workspaceRoot` and `effectiveThresholds` used for routing.
- `advisor_status` is diagnostic-only and does not rebuild. Use `advisor_rebuild({})` when status reports stale/absent/unavailable, or `advisor_rebuild({"force":true})` for an explicit live-state rebuild.
- `advisor_validate` additionally publishes `thresholdSemantics` (aggregate vs runtime) plus a prompt-safe `telemetry.outcomes.totals` block (`accepted` / `corrected` / `ignored`).
- Hook diagnostics persist to bounded JSONL sinks under the temp metrics root (`${TMPDIR}/speckit-advisor-metrics/`), so `advisor_validate` can read the sinks back across processes.

---

## 12. RETRIEVAL PRIMITIVES

The same retrieval building blocks power both hook delivery and explicit recovery:
1. `memory_match_triggers(prompt)` — Fast turn-start context
2. `memory_context({ mode: "resume", profile: "resume" })` — Continuation and compaction recovery core
3. `session_bootstrap()` / `session_resume()` — Session-oriented wrappers that layer health and structural context around resume retrieval

---

## 13. READ-ONLY GOVERNANCE DIAGNOSTICS

Two passive governance checks fire from existing surfaces and do not change recall or write stored memory data:

- `memory_health` includes `data.exclusionAudit`, derived from the hard-exclusion predicates exposed by `hybrid-search.ts`. `/doctor memory` reads that block through the existing health call and raises `hard_exclusion_risk` when deprecated-tier exclusions are risky or when an exclusion source is unclassified.
- The tool-ownership map is generated from `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` and checked by the project pre-commit hook. Drift between `mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json` and the live definitions blocks commits; the map and this document are outputs, never inputs to the ownership source of truth.

2: title: Skill Advisor Hook Reference

4: trigger_phrases:
